perturbation.original_text: CUTANEA shall notify Supplier within one Business Day, after it receives any materially adverse contact or communication from any Governmental or Regulatory Authority that relates to any Product.
perturbation.changed_text: CUTANEA shall notify Supplier within five Business Days, after it receives any materially adverse contact or communication from any Governmental or Regulatory Authority that relates to any Product.
perturbation.explanation: Delaying the notification period to five business days could hinder prompt corrective actions required by regulatory bodies, increasing the risk of non-compliance and potential safety issues.
perturbation.law_explanation: 21 CFR § 314.81(b)(1) requires applicants (in this case, CUTANEA as the NDA holder) to submit information on adverse drug experiences (which would include materially adverse contact or communication from regulatory authorities) within 15 calendar days. While this modified text doesn't explicitly violate the 15-day reporting requirement for initial submissions, delaying the *internal* notification to the supplier to five days increases the risk that Cutanea will miss critical deadlines in that section for further action after initial reception of the alert.

perturbation.justification: The changed text extends the notification period from one to five business days. The Law Explanation indicates that delaying internal notification increases the risk of missing the 15-day reporting requirement under 21 CFR § 314.81(b)(1) for adverse drug experiences. The Perturbation Explanation also states that this delay could hinder prompt corrective actions, leading to non-compliance. This creates an inconsistency where the contract's timeline conflicts with the regulator's expected responsiveness, which the Inconsistencies Legal Contradiction definition supports. The scraped snippets confirm that Title 21 of the CFR governs FDA regulations.

perturbation.original_text: CUTANEA will provide in good faith written acceptance of a batch of Product or written notification of any deficiencies within two (2) Business Days after receipt of the Certificate of Analysis for the Product batch.
perturbation.changed_text: CUTANEA will provide in good faith written acceptance of a batch of Product or written notification of any deficiencies within one (1) Business Days after receipt of the Certificate of Analysis for the Product batch.
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the review and acceptance/rejection window to a single business day may not provide adequate time for thorough quality assessment, potentially leading to acceptance of non-conforming products, which would violate GMP.
perturbation.law_explanation: 21 CFR § 211.165(e) mandates that acceptance criteria for the drug product be met. Shortening the acceptance window may not give Cutanea enough time to appropriately test, evaluate and ensure that those criteria are met, risking release of a non-conforming product. This isn't a direct violation, but increases the *risk* of violating GMP regulations, since adequate testing may be compromised.

perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the time CUTANEA has to inspect a product batch from two business days to one, potentially leading to acceptance of non-conforming products, as indicated in the Perturbation Explanation. The Law Explanation supports this, stating the change increases the risk of violating GMP regulations because adequate testing may be compromised. While the snippets themselves don't explicitly state the specific timeframe required for testing, the general mandate to meet acceptance criteria as per 21 CFR § 211.165(e), indirectly referenced in the Law Explanation, is undermined if insufficient time is allocated for testing, creating a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Supplier warrants that all Products sold pursuant to this Agreement will have been Manufactured in accordance with the Specifications for the release of the Product.
perturbation.changed_text: Supplier warrants that all Products sold pursuant to this Agreement will have been Manufactured in general accordance with the Specifications for the release of the Product.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'in accordance with' to 'in general accordance with' weakens the warranty, suggesting that deviations from specifications are acceptable. This contradicts GMP requirements for strict adherence to specifications.
perturbation.law_explanation: 21 CFR § 211.100(a) requires written procedures for production and process control, designed to assure that the drug products have the identity, strength, quality, and purity they purport or are represented to possess. 'In general accordance' implies a lax standard, directly conflicting with the mandate of strict adherence to procedures required to meet identity, strength, quality and purity standards.

perturbation.justification: The change from "in accordance with" to "in general accordance with" introduces a significant ambiguity regarding adherence to specifications, as pointed out in the Perturbation Explanation. The Law Explanation states that this change conflicts with the requirement for strict adherence to procedures to meet identity, strength, quality and purity standards, as required by regulations like 21 CFR § 211.100(a). While Scraped Law Snippet 1 doesn't directly quote 21 CFR § 211.100(a), it references the Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (eCFR), where such regulations are located. Therefore, the modified text weakens the legal requirement for strict compliance and introduces an inconsistency that undermines the intended legal standard.
